name: Backend killed before first token

injection.description: Kill one engine backend process/pod (SIGKILL, no drain) while it holds admitted requests that have not yet produced a first token.

expected_gateway_semantics: Requests on the killed backend that have not received a first upstream body byte are retried pre-first-token against another healthy backend, within the retry budget.
If the retry budget is exhausted or no healthy backend exists, the request fails with a typed error envelope (error.type=upstream_error, 5xx) carrying the request ID.
No retry ever occurs after the first token (post-first-token retry would duplicate sampled output and double-bill — Contract 1).
expected_client_visible_behavior: Either a successful response/stream (transparent pre-first-token retry; TTFT elevated) or a typed upstream_error envelope — never partial output, never duplicated output.

metrics_that_must_move.expectation: increases with stage=pre-first-token for the retried requests
goes to 0 for the killed backend within the health-check interval
error_class=upstream_error increases only for requests that exhausted the retry budget

metrics_that_must_not_move.expectation: no stage value other than pre-first-token appears at any point

abort_condition: Abort the injection if any client observes duplicated output tokens, or if the error rate of requests routed to non-injected backends exceeds 5% for 60s.
